steps.text: Dilute sodium hypochlorite (Sigma Aldrich reagent grade solution) to 6000 ± 300ppm with reagent grade water.
Test within approximately 120 min after preparation.
Cap glassware with closures.
Sterilize all glassware 2 hr in hot air oven at 180°C or steam sterilize for a minimum of 20 min at 121°C with drying cycle.
Make 3 mm right angle bend at end of 50 – 75 mm nichrome wire No. 18 B&S gage.
Place other end of the wire in a suitable holder.
Plate serial dilutions (e.g., 1.0 x 10^-6 through 1.0 x 10^-8) using pour or spread plating on TSA plates.
Add molten TSA tempered to 45-55ºC to each plate, swirl, and allow agar to solidify.
Incubate plates for 24 ± 2 hours at 36 ± 1ºC.
Determine titer.
Dilute the suspension prior to carrier inoculation to achieve a titer of 1.0 x 10^8 to 5.0 x 10^8 CFU/mL.
Transfer third system control sterile carrier to tube of subculture medium.
Place one inoculated carrier into tube of secondary subculture medium for each test to include a positive carrier control.
Incubate controls and test sample tubes together for 21 days at 36 ± 1ºC.
Perform identification confirmation on a minimum of three positive carrier sets per test, if available, using Gram stain and/or plating on TSA.
Perform additional confirmation using VITEK, API analysis or comparable method if desired.
Confirm growth from each positive carrier set.
Confirm at least 20% by Gram stain.
Produce a spore preparation according to the procedure for amended nutrient agar.
Harvest growth from plates per the method, except re-suspend pellet after final centrifugation step in approximately 100 ml aqueous (40%) ethanol.
Determine spore count by serial dilution and plating on TSA.
Adjust suspension to reach target titer if required.
Add 1.5% (w/v) Bacto-agar to un-sterilized nutrient broth.
Boil mixture until agar is dissolv
Suspend 11.5 g nutrient agar in 495 mL water.
Add 5 mL 500 ppm MnSO4:H2O.
Dissolve by boiling.
Adjust pH to 6.8 ± 0.2.
Autoclave for 15 min at 121ºC.
Pour agar into plates.
Suspend 25 g LB broth in 1 L water.
Mix well.
Adjust pH to 7.0 ± 0.2.
Dispense in bottles.
Autoclave for 15 min at 121°C.
Add 20 mL 1M NaOH to 1 L LB broth.
Mix well.
Dispense in bottles.
Autoclave for 15 min at 121°C.
Add 0.25 g of manganese sulfate to 500 mL water.
Filter sterilize for use.
Estimate the mean for each test chemical and test method combination using ANOVA.
Conduct a trend test for each method and disinfectant to determine whether the log spores per treated carrier increases significantly with known efficacy.
Code the efficacy levels as 1, 2, and 3, going from low to high efficacy.
Perform sample preparation.
Perform nucleic acid amplification with a select PCR assay.
Visualize results.
Interpret data.
Define sensitivity and LOQ characteristics beforehand for each platform if economically feasible, and take the differences into consideration in the design of a validation study.
Specify the chemistry and commercial source of reagents used for method development for each real-time PCR method.

Experimentally demonstrate that alternative sources of reagents provide equivalent performance in the method to that of the reagents from the originally specified source if designating alternative sources.
Ensure through laboratory testing that products are efficacious.
Maintain the efficacy data on file.
Submit efficacy data to support each label claim and use pattern.
Select test method to generate product efficacy data using Registration Policy Documents, DIS/TSS, or OCSPP 810.2000 guidelines.
Submit protocols to the Agency for review prior to data collection.
Obtain independent verification of the protocol in three separate laboratories.
Demonstrate statistical validity of the protocol.
Provide historical evidence (data) that the method is reproducible and relevant for its intended use.

preconditions.text: If pour plating is selected as the plating method
If fewer than three positive carrier sets are available
If both tubes are positive in carrier set, select only one tube for confirmatory testing
For test with 20 or more positive carrier sets
A neutralization confirmation test must be performed in advance or in conjunction with efficacy testing
If registering non-public health antimicrobial products
No recommended/standard test method exists, or a registrant believes a new protocol may better demonstrate efficacy of a product than the standard method.

constraints.text: Molten TSA must be tempered to 45-55ºC.
If Gram stains are performed from growth taken directly from positive tubes, the staining should be performed within 5-7 days of conducting the efficacy test.
Desirable target of the initial working suspension is 1.0 x 10^8 to 1.0 x 10^9 CFU/mL.
All manipulations of the test organism are required to be performed in accordance with biosafety practices stipulated in the institutional biosafety regulations.
Personal protective clothing or devices are recommended during the handling of disinfectants for purpose of activation, dilution, or efficacy testing.
A chemical fume hood or other containment equipment may be employed when appropriate during performing tasks with concentrated products.
Exact adherence to the method, good laboratory practices, and quality control (QC) are required for proficiency and validity of the results.
Certified biosafety cabinet (Class I or II) is recommended to maintain an aseptic work environment.
Reagent-grade water should be free of substances that interfere with analytical methods.
The formula for the standard error of the mean depends on the results of the ANOVA and the equations will be derived by the statistician.
The confidence intervals will be based on normal distribution theory.
The trend test will be a test for a significant mean slope based on a simple linear regression model.
There is a high potential for the performance of a PCR assay to be altered by different chemistries, as well as by different commercial sources of the product detection reagents.
Use of standardized, validated test methods is preferred by the Agency, but not required.
